summary.what: Extend git.Commit with CommitDate; use %aE for mailmap resolution
summary.why: Phase 2 of the v0.23.0 cross-agent debt classification plan. Both changes are load-bearing for the classifier: (1) %aE makes timbers honor .mailmap so an operator's multi-email setup doesn't get misread as foreign authorship (osprey-strike tally proved this was a 61% false-negative for Bob), (2) CommitDate gives the staleness check the right clock — AuthorDate stays put across rebase/amend so using it would silently auto-skip work the user just touched.
summary.how: Renamed conceptually but kept the existing Date field as AuthorDate (preserved for backward compat; existing callers expect it). Added CommitDate as a sibling. Updated commitFormat to emit %aE/%at/%ct in fixed positions and parseCommitFields to read them. Extracted diffstat-related code (~90 lines) into a new diffstat.go to stay under the 350-line file limit. Two new tests in commit_dates_test.go: one builds a real temp repo with a .mailmap and verifies Log() returns the canonical email; the other amends a 2020-author-dated commit and verifies Date stays in 2020 while CommitDate advances. Both follow the existing temp-repo pattern (os.Chdir + git config + git add/commit). varnamelen pushed me to rename c → commit in the divergence test loop.
